faqs.question: What spacecraft does SpaceWing use?
faqs.answer: SpaceWing operates three main spacecraft: StarHopper (suborbital), OrbitalOne (orbital missions), and MoonGlider (lunar expeditions). Each is designed and manufactured at our Mojave, California facility. Our revolutionary Wing-X design provides the smoothest re-entry experience in the industry, with wings that deploy for gentle atmospheric return.

faqs.question: How many passengers can each spacecraft carry?
faqs.answer: StarHopper carries 6 passengers plus 2 crew. OrbitalOne accommodates 12 passengers with 4 crew for transit, or up to 20 guests when docked at station. MoonGlider carries 8 passengers with 4 crew for lunar missions. All spacecraft are designed with comfort in mind—no cramped quarters here!

faqs.question: What amenities are available on board?
faqs.answer: Our spacecraft feature: large observation windows at every seat, climate-controlled cabins, real-time entertainment systems, gourmet meal service, private sleeping quarters on longer flights, hygiene facilities (yes, space toilets!), and even shower capabilities on OrbitalOne and MoonGlider. Premium cabins offer enhanced privacy and luxury finishes.

faqs.question: How does the launch experience feel?
faqs.answer: Launch is an unforgettable experience! You'll feel acceleration forces of 3-4G during ascent (like a firm pressure pushing you back in your seat), accompanied by the powerful rumble of rocket engines. The view through your window shows Earth falling away rapidly. Most travelers describe it as exhilarating rather than uncomfortable. The entire ascent lasts about 8-12 minutes.

faqs.question: What is re-entry like?
faqs.answer: Our Wing-X technology makes re-entry remarkably smooth compared to traditional capsules. You'll feel gentle deceleration forces (up to 2G) as the wings deploy and we glide through the atmosphere. Plasma outside creates a brief light show visible through special heat-resistant windows. The spacecraft then glides to a runway landing like an airplane—a far more comfortable experience than ocean splashdowns.

faqs.question: Are there windows on the spacecraft?
faqs.answer: Absolutely! Every seat has a personal window, and our spacecraft feature large communal observation domes. StarHopper has 12 windows plus a 2-meter diameter observation dome. OrbitalOne features our famous Cupola module with 360-degree views. MoonGlider includes specialized lunar observation lounges with telescope integration.

faqs.question: How do spacecraft systems stay operational?
faqs.answer: Our spacecraft feature triple-redundant systems for all critical functions—life support, navigation, and communications each have three independent backup systems. Real-time monitoring by both onboard computers and ground control ensures immediate detection of any issues. Our vehicles undergo complete inspections between every flight.

faqs.question: Can I bring electronics on board?
faqs.answer: Yes! Personal electronics are welcome after safety verification. We provide space-certified charging stations and high-speed connectivity for live streaming. Many travelers bring cameras, phones, and tablets. For safety reasons, devices must be secured during launch and re-entry. We also offer professional photography services if you prefer to leave your equipment at home.

faqs.question: How does the spacecraft land?
faqs.answer: Unlike traditional capsules that splash down in oceans, SpaceWing spacecraft land on runways like airplanes. Our Wing-X configuration deploys wings during re-entry, allowing powered or gliding flight to designated spaceports. This enables comfortable, precise landings at multiple locations worldwide and eliminates the discomfort and unpredictability of ocean recovery.

faqs.question: How often are the spacecraft maintained?
faqs.answer: Each spacecraft undergoes comprehensive inspection and maintenance between every flight. Major systems are examined, consumables replenished, and any components approaching wear limits replaced. Every 25 flights, vehicles receive complete overhauls at our manufacturing facility. Our maintenance protocols exceed all regulatory requirements and incorporate learnings from every mission.
